ai_overview: Yesterday's activity centered on mobile UX enhancements and cross-market SEO stability, with the development of a new bottom navigation bar and a major fix for XML sitemaps across 23 markets. While the team successfully moved the redesigned Sign In/Registration modals and Cloudflare Bot Management into QA, Vic Dadson is currently blocked on adding points-earning event metrics, and Dan Smullen reports that the affiliate master list for the Rollcard launch remains stalled. You should also note the cross-team focus on the new Genesis Brand Carousel, which is transitioning from design into technical planning. Please follow up with Harden Wallace regarding his request for a shared inbox upgrade to ensure team access isn't hindered. Overall, the team is making good progress on mobile conversion and AI integrations, though several UX tasks for the Learning Hub and Ace Tips remain in the backlog.

people.name: Vic Dadson
people.summary: Focused on refining the Core KPI Tracker by submitting updates for organic social metrics and fixing F2P to login conversion data. While the implementation of Cloudflare Bot Management (GDCU-8792) moved into QA, work on adding points-earning event metrics was blocked. Also prioritised the weekly report and documentation of team activities for the current period.

people.name: Jessica Dordevic
people.summary: Resolved a technical blocker involving Figma update permissions and is now shifting focus toward the design of a new Genesis Brand Carousel Component Card. Also active on 20 Apr in #gdc-product-led-growth, adding components to the Design System branch and correcting the cool-tones background colour variable (#F3F4F6) ahead of design system release.

people.name: Miguel Migneco
people.summary: Initiated a UX audit of the Ace Navigation Assistant (GDCU-8813) to identify and remove redundant UI elements following the integration of the global navigation bar. Also active in #gdc-product-led-growth on 20 Apr, sharing a working prototype for the Jump Links / Anchor Menu component (GDCU-8799) and providing design rationale for anchor link placement.

people.name: Ciara Carroll
people.summary: Focused on developing visual assets for the Learning Hub (GDCU-8736, In Progress) and managing the expansion of the Gambling.com design system. A new Jira ticket (GDCU-8893) was created for the full Gambling.com Design System project. Also posted in #gdc-pod and #gdc-product-led-growth on 20 Apr announcing the Claude Design tool integration underway — building a shared design system repository enabling conversational prompts to produce polished visual outputs and prototypes. Integration of Ace Tips into the Figma design system (GDCU-8880) remains in the backlog.

people.name: Josh Smith
people.summary: Focused on mobile UX enhancements by progressing the development of a mobile bottom navigation bar and moving a new image carousel shortcode into the QA phase. Prioritised the implementation of an Instagram headers widget for the New Zealand market while documenting user flow improvements and hygiene for several UK casino landing pages. Future work was also outlined for AI-driven sports betting tips and various UI updates currently held in the backlog. Also active in #gdc-nonen-sync clarifying image carousel shortcode implementation details with developers.

people.name: Chloe Christie
people.summary: Prioritised refinements for the Global Strategy Hub and oversaw a hotfix to ensure sign-in triggers correctly for Cosmo Spins users. Also investigated performance improvements for page timeouts while finalising a new Brand Carousel component and documenting brand removal procedures. Supplementary: posted in #gdc-nonen-sync on 20 Apr requesting non-English sign-in modal translations ahead of the GDCU-8803 release scheduled for Thursday.

people.name: Carlos Blauth
people.summary: Focused on implementing the redesigned Sign In and Registration modals across all geographic regions. The project has successfully transitioned to QA (GDCU-8803, updated 20 Apr). No additional Slack or Jira activity beyond this ticket.

people.name: Eoin McCarthy
people.summary: Limited documented activity, focusing primarily on a task to refactor scoped styles across Vue components (GDCU-8894, Backlog). This work is sitting in the backlog, indicating preparation for future technical debt improvements within the front-end architecture. No Slack activity on 20 Apr in tracked channels.

people.name: Harden Wallace
people.summary: Resolved a major SEO issue involving missing news articles in XML sitemaps across 23 markets (GDCU-8814, now Done — 6,000+ URLs restored). Troubleshot a bug where published pages were not appearing on the dev environment. Also requested a shared inbox account upgrade to improve team access while ensuring test publishes for international sites were successfully cleared.

people.name: Kelvin Yee
people.summary: No activity reported in the Viktor email. Jira shows GDCU-8823 (New Global Strategy Hub / Sub Hub Refinements) moved to 'In Product' status on 20 Apr, suggesting work was completed and sent for product review.

people.name: Milan Nikolic
people.summary: No activity in the last 24 hours. Previously noted as being on annual leave (referenced in #gdc-pdmt planning discussions on 13 Apr).

people.name: Nebojsa Stanic
people.summary: No activity reported in the Viktor email. Jira shows two active tickets on 20 Apr: GDCU-8836 (Image Carousel Shortcode, In QA — updated 15:26) and GDCU-8870 (Investigate Performance Improvements for Page Timeouts, In Progress — updated 14:07).

people.name: Novica Vukobratovic
people.summary: Focused on mobile conversion rate optimisation by developing a new bottom navigation bar (GDCU-8627, In Progress) and an Instagram headers widget for the New Zealand market (GDCU-8840, In Progress). Also managed a high-priority hotfix to ensure the sign-in prompt triggers correctly before users attempt to spin on Cosmo Spins (GDCU-8548, now In Product). Current efforts are centred on improving the mobile user experience and streamlining the registration flow across multiple regions.

people.name: Sanja Budic
people.summary: No activity reported in the Viktor email. Jira shows GDCU-8406 (Fix hreflang implementation for Slots Games Pages) moved to Done on 20 Apr — a meaningful completion. Also assigned to GDCU-8714 (Ace AI Full Football Coverage, In Progress) and GDCU-8792 (Cloudflare Bot Management, In QA).

people.name: Robin John
people.summary: No activity in the last 24 hours.

people.name: Shane McGrath
people.summary: No activity in the last 24 hours. Last active 16 Apr in #gdc-pod reporting a Sign In bug on article pages (sign-in fails on repeat after sign-out) and flagging that ACE AI daily reports had not been generated since the previous Saturday.

people.name: Dean Ryan
people.summary: No activity in the last 24 hours. Last active 15 Apr in #gdc-f2p-apps providing strategic direction for the free-to-play mobile app, recommending it be restructured as a 'Cosmo App' with a slots library and free-spins offers focus.

people.name: Michael Butlin
people.summary: Focused on documenting performance metrics by updating and creating weekly summary reports for the GDC EN market. Activity was limited to administrative reporting tasks for mid-April, with no technical blockers or development items noted.

people.name: Dan Smullen
people.summary: Completed the high-priority deployment of an automated news agent for Spotlight.Vegas and finalised the weekly SEO director report. Now shifting focus toward integrating the GetViktor AI with the Genesis CMS for automated publishing. The affiliate master list for the Rollcard launch remains blocked and is stalled.

people.name: Gabriel Cornoiu
people.summary: Focused on optimising internal processes by updating the status of editorial workflow improvements on Confluence. Supplementary: active in #gdc-pdmt on 20 Apr announcing the repurposing of the PDMT meeting — shifting it to a planning session every 3 weeks, with interim weeks used as a team weekly where no dedicated meeting exists.

people.name: Diana Thurmann
people.summary: Focused on optimising non-English markets by completing AI overviews and initiating content updates for Icelandic and Swedish casino pages. Coordinated localisation efforts through sign-up modal translations and investigated SEO improvements for Dutch payment methods while preparing regulatory frameworks for Peru. Additionally managed stakeholder reporting and inquired about testing readiness for the DACH region.

people.name: Colin Brannigan
people.summary: No activity reported in the Viktor email. Supplementary: active in #core-division-leads on 20 Apr asking the team about early experiences with the Claude Design tool, signalling interest in its potential for the division.
